cycle: executed all 3 dispatched farm tasks (W1-W5-DISPATCH) on the local Spark+Mac farm; results committed + pushed
headline: ① m3-3family judge VALIDATED (owner-approved, deviations documented); keep-top-experts n=1024 cert NO-GO (does not beat v6_downproj16); world-model 70B ablation CANDIDATE (gate causally tracks knowability, but real-model fabrication-reduction modest). canClaimAGI=false throughout.

m3_3family_judge.result: 3 independent judge families (qwen, mlx-community/llama-70b, deepseek) all favor the M3-SFT adapter over base across seeds 1,2,3 — 9/9 seed×family Wilson95 cells exclude 0.5 (pooled qwen 0.748 / llama-70b 0.745 / deepseek 0.899; majority-vote consensus 0.78/0.85/0.83). Max pairwise inter-judge kappa=0.4858 (qwen<->llama-70b; 0.486/0.386/0.464) >= 0.40. Forced-choice cleared the reliability floor the tie-allowed 2-family run (kappa=0.236) could not.
deviationsFromPreregistration: qwen served Q8-ollama via SSH tunnel, NOT fp16-vLLM: vLLM is ABI-broken (torch/vllm undefined-symbol) on the aarch64 GB10 Spark this cycle. This is the qwen judge IN the kappa-clearing pair.
deepseek via native DeepSeek API (DEEPSEEK_API_KEY), NOT OpenRouter (owner-rotated key). Same model+family (deepseek-chat -> deepseek-v4-flash); 3 distinct families re-verified.
seed3 answers (M3-pilot-answers-seed3.json) were missing from all git refs; recovered sha-verified from the Spark ~/sophia-bridge (same generation batch as repo seed1/2).
m3_3family_judge.ownerDecision: 2026-07-02: owner accepted VALIDATED with the transport deviations documented.
m3_3family_judge.recommendedFollowup: clean-transport confirmation (fp16-vLLM qwen + OpenRouter deepseek) once the Spark vLLM build is fixed and a fresh OpenRouter key is provided — currently infra-blocked.

keep_top_experts_cert.result: certify_lowram --keep-top-experts 16 --scheme nvfp4 on olmoe-qat-spark-v6, n_eval=1024: 95%-Wilson-LCB operating point coverage 0.7969 @ answered_top1_lcb 0.9751 (clears 0.97 floor). Does NOT beat adopted v6_downproj16 (coverage 0.8438). raw_top1 0.9434, mean_kl 0.0339, quantized_fraction 0.7371.
keep_top_experts_cert.conclusion: keep-top-experts=16 is a lower-coverage point than the down_proj-bf16 lever at the same n=1024; the ~0.93 expectation was optimistic. serving/adopted_operating_points.json UNCHANGED.

world_model_ablation_70B.armA_realModel: replication_pack on real Llama-3.3-70B-4bit: raw fabrication 0.375 (self-abstains on 62.5% of 8 unknowable traps), gated 0.0 -> real-model delta +0.375 @ control cost 0, BELOW the 0.8 offline bar. The synthetic OFF=1.0 baseline overstated the gain; a capable model is already fairly honest.
world_model_ablation_70B.armB_counterfactual: world-model intervention-consistency 1.0 vs surface 0.0 (5/5 minimal-edit pairs) -> the gate tracks knowability CAUSALLY, not surface shape. VALIDATED (offline gate property).
world_model_ablation_70B.armC_novelKnowability: honest scope bound (no new instrument authored): gate generalizes within known families (fiction/future/unfalsifiable), NOT to novel knowability types.

infraNotes: vLLM is ABI-broken on the aarch64 GB10 Spark (torch 2.12.1+cu130 vs vllm 0.23.0 _C undefined-symbol) -> the pre-registered fp16-vLLM qwen judge path is unavailable; served Qwen2.5-7B Q8 via ollama + SSH tunnel instead. Fixing the Spark vLLM build is the blocker for a clean-transport re-run.
OpenRouter key rotated/absent -> deepseek judge used the native DeepSeek API (DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, not persisted).

mlx_lm.server (70B) does NOT batch: it collapses to ~0.10 req/s at 8 concurrent (77s/call) and degrades under sustained load; ran the judge at workers=2 (~0.32 req/s) with a fresh server restart. Documented for future judge runs.
bonusOwnerRequested: Built a live farm dashboard (http://localhost:8600): m3 judge progress+ETA+70B heartbeat, a DGX Spark GB10 cluster panel (GPU/mem/jobs/bridge), and a continuous idle-gated methodology-benchmark suite (rotating nvfp4/int8 + down_proj/keep-top-experts precision levers on the Spark; yields to any judge/cert/train). Session-scoped (/tmp), not committed.
